name: Hybrid Facebook Lead Generation & Outreach System

parameters.jsCode:
const spamPatterns = [
  /click here/i,
  /dm me/i,
  /message me/i,
  /100% guaranteed/i,
  /make \$?\d+/i,
  /earn \$?\d+/i,
  /work from home/i,
  /crypto/i,
  /forex/i,
  /telegram/i,
  /whatsapp group/i,
  /casino/i,
  /betting/i,
  /airdrop/i,
  /giveaway/i,
  /limited offer/i,
  /affiliate/i,
  /investment opportunity/i
];

const intentPatterns = [
  /hiring/i,
  /looking for/i,
  /need(ed)?/i,
  /recommend/i,
  /recommendation/i,
  /can anyone/i,
  /anyone know/i,
  /anyone can/i,
  /who can/i,
  /help me/i,
  /someone who/i,
  /expert/i,
  /consultant/i,
  /developer/i,
  /automation/i,
  /workflow/i,
  /n8n/i,
  /make\.com/i,
  /zapier/i,
  /integrat(e|ion)/i,
  /api/i,
  /crm/i,
  /project/i,
  /freelanc(er|e)/i,
  /agency/i,
  /paid/i,
  /budget/i,
  /quote/i,
  /proposal/i,
  /looking to automate/i,
  /automate/i,
  /business automation/i
];

return items.filter(item => {
  const text = (item.json.postText || "").trim();

  if (text.length < 20) return false;

  const isSpam = spamPatterns.some(p => p.test(text));
  const hasIntent = intentPatterns.some(p => p.test(text));

  return !isSpam && hasIntent;
});

parameters.content:
# 🔍 STAGE 1: Data Collection
Runs every 12 hours (8AM & 8PM PKT).

6 keywords loop through Apify's Facebook Search Scraper:
n8n, AI automation, automation expert needed, looking for
automation developer, workflow automation, Need n8n

⚠️ Apify actor ID must stay valid — FB scraper actors break
periodically when Facebook changes anti-scraping measures.
Check Apify Store monthly.

Output gets normalized into a stable field shape (postText,
postUrl, authorName, etc.) regardless of actor's raw format.

# 🧹 STAGE 2: Filtering & Dedup
4 filters run in sequence to cut junk before AI even sees it:

1. Age filter — drops posts older than 7 days
2. Spam/intent filter — regex checks for spam patterns +
   hiring/looking-for language
3. URL normalization — strips tracking params so re-scraped
   posts don't look "new"
4. Duplicate check — matches against existing Google Sheet
   rows to avoid re-processing the same lead twice

Goal: only genuinely fresh, high-intent posts reach the AI
step. Saves Groq API calls and keeps the Sheet clean.

# Check Duplication

Duplicate check — matches against existing Google Sheet
   rows to avoid re-processing the same lead twice

# 🤖 STAGE 3: AI Qualification (Groq / Llama 3.3 70B)
⚙️ CRITICAL: Both Groq HTTP nodes MUST have Batching enabled
(Options → Batching → Items per Batch: 1, Batch Interval:
3000ms) or you'll hit 429 rate-limit errors on runs with
15+ items.

Extraction step scores each lead 1-10 + confidence 1-10.
Score >= 6 → proceeds to reply generation.
Score < 6  → logged as Rejected, saves an API call.

Hey all!
I\u2019ve been taking the time to educate myself regarding AI and automation, thought it might be valuable to share what I\u2019ve learned so far about the Do\u2019s and Don\u2019ts:

1. DONT use generative solutions (AI generated images or media) [AKA SLOP]

Honestly AI is not ready to be a graphic designer or for true generative content. There may be a day when you can use it for branding and design effectively but to be honest I think human creativity is irreplaceable in this department!

2. DO automate your administration.
AI is most effective when trained for particular tasks and procedures. You can build custom GPTs and \u201ctrain\u201d them how to follow very specific instructions. It\u2019s not creating for you it\u2019s executing.

When you leverage ai and automation for repeatable tasks, you free yourself up to focus on revenue driving behaviors. 

Here are some examples of good applications:
- invoice follow up
- estimate follow up
- automated scheduling
- expense tracking
- getting google reviews\/customer feedback.
- lead capturing (immediate follow up with inbound leads over sms)
- 24\/7 receptionist (yes this is possible)
- Cold email outbound

You can essentially automate most of your administration, and there are so many more applications.

If you want to build something like this check out tools like n8n that can integrate your tools with a custom AI. There are so many resources available online to educate yourself, but it is time intensive!

I have taken the time to learn that skill and am looking to equip a few business owners with a free automation in order to just add value and get data.

I\u2019m willing to answer any questions in the comments, DMs or over video. Will help and add value as much as I possibly can!

json.postText:
Has anyone run into the "pay-per-token" wall when building AI workflows?

I've been testing Claude and GPT-4.1 for multi-step automation pipelines, and the cost math gets ugly fast when you're chaining 10+ tool calls per run.

Google just dropped Gemma 4 two days ago under Apache 2.0 \u2014 the 12B model benchmarks close to frontier models on instruction following, and the big deal for workflow builders is: you run it locally. Zero API cost per call. No variable billing when your automation fires 500 times a day.

For prompt-heavy automation \u2014 research agents, classification chains, document processing \u2014 this changes the unit economics completely.

What I'm testing right now: running Gemma 4 locally for classification + filtering steps, then routing only the complex reasoning calls to Claude or GPT-4.1. Hybrid approach.

Anyone else experimenting with local models in their workflow stacks? What's your threshold for when API cost forces you to rethink the architecture?

json.postText:
Hey all!
I\u2019ve been taking the time to educate myself regarding AI and automation, thought it might be valuable to share what I\u2019ve learned so far about the Do\u2019s and Don\u2019ts:

1. DONT use generative solutions (AI generated images or media) [AKA SLOP]

Honestly AI is not ready to be a graphic designer or for true generative content. There may be a day when you can use it for branding and design effectively but to be honest I think human creativity is irreplaceable in this department!

2. DO automate your administration.
AI is most effective when trained for particular tasks and procedures. You can build custom GPTs and \u201ctrain\u201d them how to follow very specific instructions. It\u2019s not creating for you it\u2019s executing.

When you leverage ai and automation for repeatable tasks, you free yourself up to focus on revenue driving behaviors. 

Here are some examples of good applications:
- invoice follow up
- estimate follow up
- automated scheduling
- expense tracking
- getting google reviews\/customer feedback.
- lead capturing (immediate follow up with inbound leads over sms)
- 24\/7 receptionist (yes this is possible)
- Cold email outbound

You can essentially automate most of your administration, and there are so many more applications.

If you want to build something like this check out tools like n8n that can integrate your tools with a custom AI. There are so many resources available online to educate yourself, but it is time intensive!

I have taken the time to learn that skill and am looking to equip a few business owners with a free automation in order to just add value and get data.

I\u2019m willing to answer any questions in the comments, DMs or over video. Will help and add value as much as I possibly can!

json.postText:
Has anyone run into the "pay-per-token" wall when building AI workflows?

I've been testing Claude and GPT-4.1 for multi-step automation pipelines, and the cost math gets ugly fast when you're chaining 10+ tool calls per run.

Google just dropped Gemma 4 two days ago under Apache 2.0 \u2014 the 12B model benchmarks close to frontier models on instruction following, and the big deal for workflow builders is: you run it locally. Zero API cost per call. No variable billing when your automation fires 500 times a day.

For prompt-heavy automation \u2014 research agents, classification chains, document processing \u2014 this changes the unit economics completely.

What I'm testing right now: running Gemma 4 locally for classification + filtering steps, then routing only the complex reasoning calls to Claude or GPT-4.1. Hybrid approach.

Anyone else experimenting with local models in their workflow stacks? What's your threshold for when API cost forces you to rethink the architecture?

json.postText:
Hey all!
I\u2019ve been taking the time to educate myself regarding AI and automation, thought it might be valuable to share what I\u2019ve learned so far about the Do\u2019s and Don\u2019ts:

1. DONT use generative solutions (AI generated images or media) [AKA SLOP]

Honestly AI is not ready to be a graphic designer or for true generative content. There may be a day when you can use it for branding and design effectively but to be honest I think human creativity is irreplaceable in this department!

2. DO automate your administration.
AI is most effective when trained for particular tasks and procedures. You can build custom GPTs and \u201ctrain\u201d them how to follow very specific instructions. It\u2019s not creating for you it\u2019s executing.

When you leverage ai and automation for repeatable tasks, you free yourself up to focus on revenue driving behaviors. 

Here are some examples of good applications:
- invoice follow up
- estimate follow up
- automated scheduling
- expense tracking
- getting google reviews\/customer feedback.
- lead capturing (immediate follow up with inbound leads over sms)
- 24\/7 receptionist (yes this is possible)
- Cold email outbound

You can essentially automate most of your administration, and there are so many more applications.

If you want to build something like this check out tools like n8n that can integrate your tools with a custom AI. There are so many resources available online to educate yourself, but it is time intensive!

I have taken the time to learn that skill and am looking to equip a few business owners with a free automation in order to just add value and get data.

I\u2019m willing to answer any questions in the comments, DMs or over video. Will help and add value as much as I possibly can!
